mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate weakening demand fundamentals, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with demand proxies (Rank IC > 0.05) or individual series fail to predict returns in isolation (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 10-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary tightening cycle that raises equity discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or FEDFUNDS fails to explain return variation (R-squared < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or INDPRO's predictive power vanishes in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in unemployment (UNRATE) and decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a severe labor market shock that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive correlation with labor proxies (Rank IC > 0.05) or individual series fail to predict returns in isolation (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 11300 billion dollars indicates economic contraction that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with GDP (Rank IC > 0.05) or GDP fails to explain return variation (R-squared < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% combined with stable interest rates (FEDFUNDS) signals unanchored inflation expectations that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with inflation-interest rate proxies (Rank IC > 0.05) or individual series fail to predict returns in isolation (p > 0.10).
